quote_of_day.text: I still don't understand why Animal was hated so much. I enjoyed it more than Dhurandhar, Animal had more literally me moments with that swastika scene. Animal feels more timeless, and a very very very good story on toxic father etc situations. It was a fantastic movie
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A toxic high pressure system of racist vitriol dominated the board this morning, with a post comparing Africans to 'homo erectus' and claiming they have '0 impulse control'. This sparked a brief but intense squall of replies.

A front of political criticism moved in by mid-morning, as an anon criticized Indian leadership, sparking a debate that drew a moderate amount of discussion.

The criticism focused on the leaders' priorities, with the anon arguing they should focus on building factories and investing in R&D rather than engaging in caste-based politics.

The afternoon saw a scattered drizzle of entertainment news, with a post praising Ranbir Kapoor's 'Animal' and sparking a debate about the film's merits.

Meanwhile, two smaller threads - one suggesting that anons shill a website on social media, and another inquiring about a doxxing incident and the state of the board - added to the overall sense of overcast skies, with a general feeling of tension and unease.

articles.title: Anon compares Africans to 'homo erectus', claims they have '0 impulse control'
articles.body:
Anon expressed fear that Africans, if they flood other countries, would bring chaos due to their supposed lack of impulse control, likening them to 'homo erectus'.

Another anon chimed in, stating that African leaders are often incompetent, corrupt, and have taken poor decisions. One respondent fantasized about a sexual encounter with Candace Owens, imagining her in a blue saree and chanting 'Jai Bhim slogans'.

Anon also discussed the Israel-Africa dynamic, mentioning that Israel has 'raped nations for less' and highlighting their actions in the Entebbe raid.

A claim was made that one-third of the African genome is 'non-sapien', with some anons suggesting that Africans are not fully human.

Anon also criticized 'defending niggers' as a low-caste endeavor, and several respondents shared derogatory views on African intelligence and societal structures.

The discussion included assertions that Africans have 'semi-functioning nation states and militaries' but are unable to manage their own affairs effectively.

The thread also touched on the topic of colonialism and its perceived impact on Africa, with one anon stating that Africans 'reverted to their primal monkey state' after decolonization.

The tone of the discussion was marked by extreme hostility and racism towards Africans.

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Indian Leadership, Sparks Debate
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion, stating that if Indian leaders spent more energy on building factories, manufacturing, and R&D, rather than focusing on "bhangi reservation scams, caste votebank warfare, and 'hisab chukta' politics," India wouldn't be a laughingstock still importing everything today.

One respondent argued that the issue runs deeper, with demography being the destiny, and current leaders have no incentive to change.

Another anon suggested that if India paused its reservation system, it could allocate funds to pressing issues like cleaning the Yamuna river and improving sewer designs.

The conversation turned to India's military capabilities, with an anon stating that modernizing and expanding the military to sustain offensive operations would require significant investment, estimated at around $100 billion over a decade.

Some argued that improving the military is crucial, while others emphasized the need to address basic infrastructure issues like sewage management.

The discussion also touched on the idea that the Indian government might be infiltrated and backed by external forces to keep the country unstable.

Anon also debated the effectiveness of current military equipment and training, with some pointing out that even with modern equipment, the military's performance is subpar.

The thread also featured several harsh exchanges, with one anon making derogatory comments about certain castes, prompting a response that criticized the repetitive nature of such comments.

The conversation reflects a range of perspectives on India's development, leadership, and military capabilities.

articles.title: Anon Praises Ranbir Kapoor's 'Animal', Debate Ensues
articles.body:
Anon expressed admiration for the movie 'Animal', stating they enjoyed it more than 'Dhurandhar' and appreciated its portrayal of toxic father-son relationships. The original poster (OP) mentioned the film's swastika scene as a particularly memorable moment.

Another anon responded, criticizing the OP for not understanding why 'Animal' was hated, suggesting that people who dislike popular films often do so to appear intelligent. This respondent also expressed disappointment with the movie's execution despite its promising concept.

The discussion took a turn when an anon brought up the topic of caste, stating that the board needs to address issues with certain castes, specifically mentioning dalits and OBCs, and their perceived tendencies.

This comment was met with a mix of reactions, including one anon suggesting that such discussions should be contained to prevent them from becoming repetitive and hateful.

Some anons shared their opinions on Bollywood and caste dynamics, with one stating that hating Bollywood is a low-caste trait, while another claimed that dalits are often portrayed inaccurately in films.

The conversation also touched on the movie 'Animal' itself, with one anon praising its unique storytelling and entertainment value. The debate continued with anons discussing the film's protagonist and its depiction of certain communities.

One respondent criticized Ranbir Kapoor's acting, while another appreciated the movie's portrayal of a powerful father-son relationship.

articles.title: Anon Suggests Shilling Website on Social Media
articles.body: Anon posted about shilling the website on Instagram and Reddit to increase traffic. Another anon replied that they would try to improve the site's UI and then consider shilling on other platforms. A separate suggestion was made to pay for Google ads or Reddit ads to attract new users, with one anon noting that old users are tired of debating the same topics.

articles.title: Motivanon's doxxing and the state of the board
articles.body:
Anon asked about what happened with BharatChan and why it was "gayush"-ed. Another anon replied that the owner had issues with reports, raids, and internal conflicts, leading to him quitting.

Anon explained that the owner, motivanon, got doxxed after mentioning a workplace conversation and his last name in a thread. This led to threats of doxxing his Japanese workplace and harassment.

Some anons discussed the aftermath, with one saying they regretted doxxing him and another mentioning that motivanon had been trying to contribute but was met with hostility.

Anon also claimed motivanon had an interest in the occult and was working on a game.
